title: Diabetic eye screening result review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports recording bilateral visual acuity, image status, grading, screening outcome and result letters sent to the person and relevant clinicians.
examination.rationale: The exact screening-test and result section supports associated symptoms, relevant negatives, focused examination if actually assessed and clinician-reviewed result documentation without assigning a retinal grade.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports recording bilateral visual acuity, image status, grading, screening outcome and result letters sent to the person and relevant clinicians.

source.title: Diabetic eye screening pathway requirements specification

exact_section.heading: Screening test, grading and result communication

investigations.text: Supports recording eligibility, attendance, prior pathway status, information sharing and coordination with primary care, diabetology, hospital eye and maternity services.
investigations.rationale: The exact NHS pathway section supports interval and patient-concern documentation plus review of previous endocrine or screening correspondence, without inferring eligibility or attendance.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording eligibility, attendance, prior pathway status, information sharing and coordination with primary care, diabetology, hospital eye and maternity services.

source.title: Diabetic eye screening pathway requirements specification

exact_section.heading: End-to-end pathway and information sharing
exact_section.locator: End-to-end pathway; section 1 Before screening test

referral.text: Supports recording clinician-confirmed surveillance, ungradable-image, digital-surveillance or hospital-eye-service referral status without autonomously assigning a grade or destination.
referral.rationale: The exact surveillance and referral pathway supports recording a clinician-entered plan, safety discussion and arranged follow-up only when already determined; it does not generate referral or surveillance timing.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports recording clinician-confirmed surveillance, ungradable-image, digital-surveillance or hospital-eye-service referral status without autonomously assigning a grade or destination.

source.title: Diabetic eye screening pathway requirements specification

exact_section.heading: Surveillance and hospital-eye-service pathway
exact_section.locator: section 2 Screening test; surveillance and referral requirements

source_limitations: Screening date, laterality, visual acuity, image quality, retinal and macular grade, result letter, pregnancy, surveillance status, interpretation, referral and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
